Responsible by Design

Impact &
Sustainability

Economically viable. Environmentally sustainable. Socially responsible. Every claim at Hacienda Labu is independently verified, annually audited, and backed by internationally recognised standards.

Certifications

Every claim independently
verified. Every year.

FSC® Forest Stewardship Council
Plantation & Sawmill
C108572 · Since 2013 · Annual audit

Full chain of custody from plantation to finished product. Both our teak plantation and sawmill are certified — the complete production system, not just a single step.

CertificateFSC® C108572
ScopePlantation + sawmill — full chain of custody
EUDRFully compliant — all parcels geolocation-verified
Rainforest Alliance · UTZ Standard
Cacao Division
UTZ · Cacao only · Annual audit

Annual audit across sustainable agriculture, fair labour, and environmental protection. Applied to our 1,100 ha of shade-grown cacao in the asocio system.

Covers1,100 ha cacao — shade-grown asocio system
StandardRainforest Alliance (UTZ)
FrequencyAnnual independent audit
Verra Verified Carbon Standard
Carbon Credits
VCS ID 2365 · Validated 2021

63,782 VCUs verified across five vintage periods. ARR methodology — prevention-first carbon removal on previously degraded cattle land.

Project IDVCS 2365 — registry.verra.org
MethodologyARR — prevention, not post-emission offset
VCUs issued63,782 across 5 vintages
IFC Performance Standards
Environmental & Social
PS1 · PS6 · PS7 · Full compliance

Environmental, biodiversity, and indigenous peoples performance standards. Teak exports are EUDR-compliant for all European markets.

StandardsPS1 · PS6 (Biodiversity) · PS7 (Indigenous)
EUDRCompliant — not a certification
Biological Audit
Olman Murillo PhD
TEC Costa Rica · Annual

Annual independent biological audit covering biodiversity monitoring, conservation programme progress, species inventories, and ecosystem health across all 5,024 hectares.

ScopeBiodiversity · Conservation · Species monitoring
StandardIFC Performance Standard PS6
Financial Audit
PricewaterhouseCoopers
PwC · Annual · IFRS

Annual financial statements audit by PricewaterhouseCoopers. Investor-grade financial transparency across all Hacienda Labu operations.

AuditorPricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC)
StandardInternational Financial Reporting Standards
Biodiversity

A living ecosystem,
not just a plantation.

Close to 1,576 hectares of conservation area buffer the Bosawás Biosphere Reserve — the second-largest tropical rainforest in the western hemisphere. 920+ species of flora and fauna documented across protection areas.

🌿450Plant species
🦜266Bird species
🐾34Mammal species
🦎47Reptile species
🐸16Amphibian species
🦋72Butterfly species

Keel-Billed Toucan
Ramphastos sulfuratus
Spider Monkeys
Ateles geoffroyi
Howler Monkey
Alouatta palliata
Three-Toed Sloth
Bosawás Conservation Area
Giant Anteater
Myrmecophaga tridactyla
Conservation Area · 1,576 Ha · Bosawás Buffer
Conservation Programme

1,576 ha protecting
Bosawás Reserve.

Our conservation area forms a living buffer between Hacienda Labu's productive land and the Bosawás UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Several rivers and streams flow through the estate, supporting aquatic and riparian ecosystems.

🌲
Native Species in Conservation Area
Mahogany (Swietenia macrophylla), Guapinol (Hymenaea courbaril), Royal Cedar (Dalbergia stevensonii), and Marango (Moringa oleifera) planted in riparian and buffer areas — restoring habitat and extending wildlife corridors.
🔭
Annual Monitoring
Biological audit conducted by Olman Murillo PhD, TEC Costa Rica. Air and water quality monitoring. Rescue and reintegration programme for endemic species.
🌱
550-Ha Restoration Programme
Active ecological restoration programme. 55 ha replanted per year with native species — enriching degraded buffer areas and increasing biodiversity scores annually.
🌳
400,000+ Plants Donated
Hacienda Labu has donated over 400,000 plants to Nicaragua's national reforestation campaign — extending impact well beyond our own estate boundaries.
The Transformation
5,024 ha of degraded
land restored to forest.

From cattle pasture to productive agroforestry in 15 years. Target: 4,500 ha of full forest cover by 2037 through the continuous asocio planting cycle — then replanting begins again.

Before & After

Cattle land
transformed.

Before
After
2010
Planting begins — degraded cattle land in Siuna and Bonanza starts its transformation.
2013
FSC® certified — both plantation and sawmill receive chain-of-custody certification.
2021
Verra VCS validated — Carbon project ID 2365 validated using ARR methodology.
2028
First final harvest → replant → the cycle begins again, basically forever.
2037
4,500 ha forest cover — target for full forest coverage across all properties.
The Asocio System

Two canopy layers.
One integrated system.

The asocio method creates a functioning multi-layer forest ecosystem rather than a monoculture. Each layer plays a role in soil health, water regulation, and carbon sequestration.

☀️
Upper Canopy — Certified Teak
Plantation teak (Tectona grandis) provides shade, wind protection, and the primary carbon sequestration layer. FSC®-certified. Harvested on rotation — continuous canopy maintained.
🍫
Mid Canopy — Cacao
Cacao (Theobroma cacao) benefits from shade-regulated microclimate — stable humidity, lower pest pressure, deeper flavour complexity. Rainforest Alliance certified. No other operation in Nicaragua does this at commercial scale.
💧
Watershed Protection
Multiple rivers and streams flow through the estate. Forest cover protects watershed integrity, reduces erosion, and maintains water quality for downstream communities.
Community

People at the centre
of everything we do.

Hacienda Labu operates in Siuna and Bonanza — one of Nicaragua's most economically marginalised regions. Our commitment is built into how we operate, not added as a programme.

Community · Siuna & Bonanza
Women's Team
Local Employment
450+Employees/yr
90%From Siuna & Bonanza
510+Students
3Partner schools
👷
Direct Employment
450+ formal employment positions per year — 90% from Siuna and Bonanza, 100% Nicaraguan. Stable contracts, competitive wages, and career development in one of Nicaragua's most economically marginalised regions.
🏫
Aula Verde — Green Classroom
Environmental education programme in three community schools neighbouring our main operations in Siuna — directly benefiting 510+ students with hands-on agroforestry and environmental science education.
🏥
Health, Sports & Infrastructure
Economic support for community health initiatives, sports facilities, local hospital support, and public road maintenance. Open communication channels — suggestion and complaint mechanisms in every neighbouring community.
📚
Education Support
Donations of school supplies — books, backpacks, and educational materials — to schools around our properties in Siuna and Bonanza. Supporting the families of our workforce.
Indigenous Community Relations

Mayangna Sauni Arungka
(Matumbak Territory)

Our operations in Bonanza sit within the ancestral territory of the Mayangna — nine communities of the Panamahka-speaking Mayangna people. Our relationship is built on formal legal recognition, respect for their cosmovisión and governance authorities, and active cooperation on shared priorities.

AgreementFive-year cooperation agreement 2021–2025 with GTI-Matumbak — institutional strengthening, property rights, community development, reforestation, and training for women and youth.
Communities9 communities of Matumbak Territory. Closest to operations: Pansuhwas, Mukuswas, and Ispayulilna. Annual assemblies with all 9.
StandardIFC Performance Standard PS7 — Indigenous Peoples. Annual assemblies. Grievance mechanism in every community.
Landmark · March 2024
Transfer of Finca Miranda

The first voluntary company-to-indigenous-community land transfer under Nicaragua's Law 445. MLR Forestal transferred 113 ha in Bonanza to the Matumbak Territory — accompanied by Landesa, supported by FMO and Finnfund.

113Ha transferred
9Communities
4,500People
#1First Law 445

18 ha sun cacao · 34 ha cacao under teak · 5 ha teak · 57 ha conservation. MLR continues technical support for Matumbak to operate the plantation sustainably.

Our People — What We Do For Them
Formal Contracts
Stable contracts — not seasonal. Social security contributions for every employee.
Competitive Wages
The highest-paying formal employer in the Siuna area. Reliable income anchoring families.
Career Development
Field technicians advance to supervisors and department heads. University degree support.
Equal Opportunities
Gender equality integrated into hiring. Women hold leadership roles across all divisions.
Environmental Education
All employees participate in Aula Verde — understanding the ecosystem they work within.
Open Channels
Grievance and suggestion channels — WhatsApp, phone, email, and community boxes.

SDG Alignment

Four goals.
One integrated estate.

08
Decent Work & Economic Growth
450+ formal employees in Siuna and Bonanza. 100% Nicaraguan, 90% local. Stable wages, skills development, and women's leadership programmes.
13
Climate Action
63,782 VCUs verified. 1.1M+ tonnes estimated lifetime. ARR prevention methodology. Verra VCS 2365 validated 2021. Annual PwC and biological audits.
15
Life on Land
1,576 ha conservation buffering Bosawás UNESCO Reserve. 550-ha restoration. 450 plant species and 266 bird species monitored. 400,000+ plants donated nationally.
17
Partnerships for the Goals
FSC® · Rainforest Alliance · Verra VCS · IFC Standards · Annual PwC audit · Annual biological audit · Landesa · Impact finance partners.
